items.title: Needle: Inside a Modular Crypto-Stealing C2 That Left Its Keys in the Malware
items.description: A modular Malware-as-a-Service crypto-stealing platform called Needle has been discovered actively targeting cryptocurrency wallets through two main attack vectors: a browser extension spoofer targeting MetaMask, Phantom, and Trust Wallet, and a Rust-based desktop agent impersonating Exodus, Trezor, and Ledger applications. The campaign compromised 1,932 victims, including 111 browser extension us
items.ai_summary: The Needle malware campaign is a modular crypto-stealing platform that targets cryptocurrency wallets through two primary attack vectors: a browser extension spoofer and a Rust-based desktop agent. The browser extension spoofer targets popular wallets such as MetaMask, Phantom, and Trust Wallet, while the desktop agent impersonates well-known applications like Exodus, Trezor, and Ledger. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement strict browser extension validation, ensuring that only trusted extensions are installed, and verify the authenticity of desktop applications by checking digital signatures and verifying the publisher. Additionally, users should be cautious when installing browser extensions and desktop applications, and monitor their cryptocurrency wallets for suspicious activity. By taking these specific remediation steps, defenders can reduce the risk of compromise from the Needle malware campaign.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1176| B[Browser Extension]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    C -->|T1588| D[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Honeypot reveals botnet exploiting scriptText to launch DDoS attacks on game servers
items.description: Analysts observed attackers exploiting a Jenkins honeypot to deploy a new DDoS botnet targeting video game servers. Leveraging Jenkins scriptText abuse, the threat actors achieved remote code execution by sending malicious Groovy scripts to intentionally misconfigured instances with weak passwords. The multi-platform payload targets both Windows and Linux systems, deploying malware that evades det
items.ai_summary: The threat actors are exploiting misconfigured Jenkins instances with weak passwords to deploy a DDoS botnet targeting video game servers. The attack vector involves sending malicious Groovy scripts to achieve remote code execution, leveraging the scriptText feature in Jenkins. This multi-platform payload targets both Windows and Linux systems, deploying malware that evades detection. To remediate, defenders should ensure that Jenkins instances are properly configured with strong passwords and that the scriptText feature is restricted or disabled, and update to the latest version of Jenkins, which includes security patches for similar vulnerabilities. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to T1583.005, T1190, T1059, T1550, T1110.001, and T1498, and implement robust network denial of service protections to prevent DDoS attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1550| D[Use Alternate Authentication Material]:::tactic
    D -->|T1498| E[Network Denial of Service]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Active Exploitation of Check Point VPN Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-50751)
items.description: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability affecting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability exploits a logic flaw in certificate validation within the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protocol, allowing attackers to establish VPN sessions without valid passwords. Exploitation has been observed since May 7, 2026, targeting several
items.ai_summary: The Check Point VPN authentication bypass vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50751, is a critical flaw in the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protocol that allows attackers to establish VPN sessions without valid passwords. This vulnerability affects Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments, and its exploitation has been observed since May 7, 2026, targeting several organizations. To mitigate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately upgrade to the latest Check Point VPN software version, which deprecates the vulnerable IKEv1 protocol in favor of the more secure IKEv2. Additionally, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication and monitor VPN connection logs for suspicious activity. By taking these specific remediation steps, defenders can prevent the exploitation of this vulnerability and protect their networks from unauthorized access.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1078| C[Valid Accounts]:::tactic
    C -->|T1212| D[Exploitation for Credential Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1021| E[Remote Services]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: From Fake Amazon Security Alert to HarborWatch Agent: ClickFix Delivery of a Custom Monitoring RAT
items.description: A sophisticated phishing campaign exploits Amazon's brand reputation through spoofed security alerts to deliver HarborWatch Agent, a custom remote access trojan. The attack chain begins with emails impersonating Amazon security notifications about suspicious account activity, directing victims to lookalike domains. Users are presented with fake CAPTCHA verification pages that employ ClickFix socia
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a sophisticated phishing campaign that exploits Amazon's brand reputation by sending spoofed security alerts to potential victims, directing them to lookalike domains. The attack chain utilizes T1566 phishing tactics, where users are presented with fake CAPTCHA verification pages that employ ClickFix social engineering techniques. If exploited, victims may inadvertently install HarborWatch Agent, a custom remote access trojan, allowing threat actors to gain unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, educate users about phishing attacks, and monitor for suspicious domain activity related to T1583.001. Additionally, defenders should ensure that all remote access software is up-to-date and configured to follow best practices, as outlined in T1219.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1583.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1055| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Technical Analysis of MLTBackdoor
items.description: In May 2026, a new malware family named MLTBackdoor was identified, likely leveraged by ransomware-related threat actors to establish footholds for lateral movement. Delivered through multi-stage ClickFix infection chains targeting automotive-related web pages, this backdoor employs sophisticated obfuscation techniques including Mixed Boolean-Arithmetic and Control Flow Flattening. MLTBackdoor fea
items.ai_summary: The MLTBackdoor malware family, identified in May 2026, is a sophisticated threat that employs obfuscation techniques such as Mixed Boolean-Arithmetic and Control Flow Flattening to evade detection. This malware is delivered through multi-stage ClickFix infection chains targeting automotive-related web pages, allowing threat actors to establish footholds for lateral movement. If exploited, MLTBackdoor can lead to significant real-world impact, including data encryption and potential ransomware attacks, affecting organizations in the automotive sector. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust network monitoring, focusing on detecting unusual remote service activity and multi-stage channel communications, and ensure all remote access software is up-to-date and securely configured. Specifically, defenders should prioritize patching any vulnerabilities related to remote services and access software, and consider implementing a web application firewall to filter traffic to automotive-related web pages.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1104| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Phishing Attacks Leverage TikTok, Instagram Reels
items.description: Threat actors are exploiting short-form video platforms like TikTok and Instagram Reels to conduct social engineering attacks. Two distinct campaign methods have been identified: professional-looking fake tutorials with AI-generated voiceovers promising free premium software, and casual videos showcasing premium features to generate engagement through comments. Both approaches direct victims to ma
items.ai_summary: Threat actors are leveraging short-form video platforms such as TikTok and Instagram Reels to conduct social engineering attacks, specifically phishing campaigns, identified as T1566. The attacks involve creating professional-looking fake tutorials with AI-generated voiceovers that promise free premium software, or casual videos showcasing premium features to generate engagement through comments. These approaches direct victims to malicious links, where they are tricked into divulging sensitive information or downloading malware, potentially leading to user execution (T1204) of malicious code. To remediate, defenders should educate users about the risks of interacting with unsolicited videos and links on social media platforms, and implement robust email and web filters to block phishing attempts. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing multi-factor authentication to mitigate the impact of compromised credentials.

    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059| D[Persistence]:::tactic

    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: PHISH ALERT: Press Play for Compromise — Voicemail Phishing Kit Bundles SSO Hijacking, Credential Theft, and RMM Delivery
items.description: An advanced voicemail-themed phishing campaign is utilizing HTML attachments to hijack Microsoft 365 sessions through silent OAuth exploitation. Emails arrive spoofing legitimate businesses with fake voicemail notifications containing embedded HTML files. When victims click the play button, the kit triggers a rogue OAuth 2.0 request using the prompt=none parameter to steal authentication tokens fr
items.ai_summary: The PHISH ALERT campaign utilizes HTML attachments to hijack Microsoft 365 sessions through silent OAuth exploitation, specifically targeting users with fake voicemail notifications. When victims click the play button, the kit triggers a rogue OAuth 2.0 request using the prompt=none parameter to steal authentication tokens, allowing attackers to gain unauthorized access to sensitive information. This campaign is particularly concerning as it bypasses traditional phishing defenses, and affected users may not even realize their credentials have been compromised. To remediate, defenders should implement conditional access policies in Microsoft 365, such as requiring multi-factor authentication for all users, and ensure that the 'prompt=none' parameter is properly validated in OAuth 2.0 requests. Additionally, users should be educated on the risks of interacting with suspicious emails and attachments, and should be instructed to report any unusual activity to their IT department immediately.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1212| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Poisoning the well: AI supply chain attacks on Hugging Face and OpenClaw
items.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting AI distribution platforms like Hugging Face and ClawHub to deliver malware by embedding malicious code within models, datasets, and agent extensions. Over 575 malicious skills across 13 developer accounts were identified in the OpenClaw ecosystem, targeting Windows and macOS with trojans, cryptominers, and AMOS stealer. Attackers abuse trust relationships betw
items.ai_summary: The threat actors are exploiting AI distribution platforms like Hugging Face and ClawHub by embedding malicious code within models, datasets, and agent extensions, resulting in the delivery of malware such as trojans, cryptominers, and AMOS stealer to Windows and macOS systems. This supply chain attack, identified as T1195, has led to the discovery of over 575 malicious skills across 13 developer accounts in the OpenClaw ecosystem. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement strict validation and verification processes for AI models and datasets, and ensure that all dependencies and extensions are from trusted sources. Additionally, developers should prioritize secure coding practices and regularly audit their code for potential vulnerabilities. By taking these specific remediation steps, defenders can reduce the risk of compromise and protect their systems from these targeted attacks.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: Ransomware Analysis: Go Binary and Fast Encryption
items.description: The Gentlemen is a Ransomware-as-a-Service operation, tracked as Storm-2697, that emerged in mid-2025 after splitting from Qilin ransomware following a payment dispute. Operating as a highly structured syndicate with at least 9 core operators, the group has compromised over 1,570 organizations across 70+ countries, with approximately 71-78% paying ransoms and never appearing on public leak sites.
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen ransomware, tracked as Storm-2697, is a highly structured Ransomware-as-a-Service operation that emerged in mid-2025. This threat has compromised over 1,570 organizations across 70+ countries, with approximately 71-78% of victims paying ransoms. The attack vector involves obtaining capabilities through T1588, followed by data encryption for impact via T1486, resulting in significant financial losses for affected organizations. To remediate, defenders should focus on implementing robust backup and disaster recovery systems, ensuring that all sensitive data is encrypted and inaccessible to potential attackers, and conducting regular security audits to identify vulnerabilities. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing a ransomware-specific incident response plan, including procedures for rapid containment and eradication of the threat.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    C -->|T1070| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: SilabRAT, What's Your Power?
items.description: SilabRAT is an advanced Remote Access Trojan offered as Malware-as-a-Service on Darkweb forums since late 2025, developed by threat actor o1oo1 and sold for $5,000 monthly. This financially-motivated tool focuses on credential theft and cryptocurrency operations, featuring Hidden Virtual Network Computing for invisible remote control, browser profile cloning to bypass session protections, and auto
items.ai_summary: The SilabRAT Remote Access Trojan, developed by threat actor o1oo1, is a highly advanced malware tool sold as Malware-as-a-Service on Darkweb forums for $5,000 monthly. It primarily targets credential theft and cryptocurrency operations, utilizing Hidden Virtual Network Computing for stealthy remote control and browser profile cloning to bypass session protections. If exploited, SilabRAT can lead to significant financial losses and unauthorized access to sensitive information, affecting individuals and organizations alike. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network monitoring, utilize two-factor authentication, and regularly update their security software to the latest versions, such as ensuring VNC clients are updated to the latest patch levels. Additionally, users should be cautious when accessing cryptocurrency platforms and avoid using public Wi-Fi for sensitive transactions.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1021.005| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Remote Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1021.005| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-10197: A vulnerability was detected in Assimp up to 6.0.4. Affected is the function glT...
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in Assimp up to 6.0.4. Affected is the function glTF2Importer::ImportEmbeddedTextures in the library code/AssetLib/glTF2/glTF2Importer.cpp of the component TF File Handler. The manipulation results in null pointer dereference. The attack is only possible with local access. The exploit is now public and may be used. It is advisable to implement a patch to correct this i
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10197 is a vulnerability in the Assimp library up to version 6.0.4, specifically affecting the glTF2Importer::ImportEmbeddedTextures function in the TF File Handler component. This vulnerability can be exploited through local access, resulting in a null pointer dereference. If exploited, this could lead to crashes or potentially allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code, although the impact is somewhat mitigated by the requirement for local access. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update Assimp to version 6.0.5 or later, which includes a patch for this issue. Additionally, developers using Assimp in their projects should ensure they are using a version of the library that includes this fix.

    D -->|T1529| E[System Discovery]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2026-8796: Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via...
items.description:
Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via crafted input.

In Perl/Decoder/srl_decoder.c, srl_read_object() and srl_read_hash() process a COPY tag, a back-reference whose target byte the decoder re-decodes as a fresh tag. When that target byte matches the SHORT_BINARY pattern (an inline string whose length is encoded in the low bits of the tag), the resulting
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8796 is a heap out-of-bounds read vulnerability in Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by crafting malicious input, allowing them to read arbitrary memory locations. This can lead to information disclosure or potentially even code execution. To remediate this issue, update Sereal::Decoder to version 5.005 or later, and ensure that the Perl interpreter is configured to use the latest security patches. Additionally, consider implementing input validation and sanitization mechanisms to prevent similar attacks in the future.

items.title: CVE-2026-10194: A weakness has been identified in OFFIS DCMTK 3.7.0. This affects the function D...
items.description: A weakness has been identified in OFFIS DCMTK 3.7.0. This affects the function DcmQueryRetrieveIndexDatabaseHandle::deleteOldestImages of the file dcmqrdb/libsrc/dcmqrdbi.cc of the component dcmqrscp. Executing a manipulation can lead to heap-based buffer overflow. The attack may be launched remotely. This patch is called 0f78a4ef6f645ea5530166e445e5436a5de58e75. A patch should be applied to remed
items.ai_summary: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability (CVE-2026-10194) has been identified in OFFIS DCMTK 3.7.0, specifically in the function DcmQueryRetrieveIndexDatabaseHandle::deleteOldestImages of the file dcmqrdb/libsrc/dcmqrdbi.cc of the component dcmqrscp. This weakness can be exploited remotely, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code on the affected system. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to privilege escalation or data breach. To remediate this issue, defenders should apply the patch 0f78a4ef6f645ea5530166e445e5436a5de58e75 to OFFIS DCMTK 3.7.0. Additionally, it is recommended to update to the latest version of OFFIS DCMTK, which is 3.7.1, to ensure the vulnerability is fully patched.

items.title: CVE-2026-10193: A security flaw has been discovered in OFCMS up to 1.1.3. The impacted element i...
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in OFCMS up to 1.1.3. The impacted element is the function Query of the file ofcms-admin\src\main\java\com\ofsoft\cms\admin\controller\ComnController.java of the component ComnController. Performing a manipulation of the argument system.user.query results in sql injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10193 is a SQL injection vulnerability in OFCMS up to version 1.1.3, specifically in the ComnController's Query function. An attacker can manipulate the system.user.query argument to inject malicious SQL code, which can be initiated remotely. This exploit has been publicly released and may be used to extract sensitive data or disrupt the application. To remediate, update to OFCMS version 1.1.4 or later, and ensure the system.user.query argument is properly sanitized. Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent future attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-10192: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda W12 3.0.0.7(4763). The affected element ...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda W12 3.0.0.7(4763). The affected element is the function set_local_time_0 of the file /bin/httpd. Such manipulation of the argument Time leads to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10192 is a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in Tenda W12 3.0.0.7(4763) that can be exploited remotely. The vulnerability is located in the function set_local_time_0 of the file /bin/httpd, which can be manipulated by passing a malicious Time argument. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to arbitrary code execution, allowing an attacker to gain control of the device. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to Tenda W12 3.0.0.8(4764) or later, and ensure that the httpd service is not running with elevated privileges. Additionally, users should implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block any incoming traffic to the /bin/httpd file.

items.title: The 5 Best Practices for Secure Identity Verification
items.description: Attackers are increasingly bypassing weak authentication through phishing, MFA fatigue, and service desk social engineering. Specops Software breaks down five best practices for stronger identity verification and access security. [...]

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines
items.description: As attackers increasingly favor stolen credentials over exploits, infostealers have become a primary source of access for ransomware and other cybercrime operations. The post Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation
items.description: Cyera is positioned as one of the most valuable privately held cybersecurity firms in the world with total funding topping $2 billion. The post Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft patches Exchange Server zero-day exploited in attacks
items.description: Microsoft has patched an actively exploited Exchange Server vulnerability that allows threat actors to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks targeting Outlook Web Access users. [...]

items.title: Microsoft ships largest Patch Tuesday on record, with one bug under active attack
items.description: The release comes after Microsoft’s security leadership acknowledged last month that AI tools are driving a surge in vulnerability discovery across the industry.

items.title: Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding
items.description: In the post-Mythos era, the company’s platform helps organizations enforce security controls across environments. The post Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers
items.description: Claroty researchers have analyzed the security of Vertiv UPS network cards and the Trane Tracer SC+ HVAC controller. The post Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review
items.description: Learn more about protecting against unmonitored use of generative AI (Shadow AI) in business units and building and enforcing AI governance frameworks. The post CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Windows Zero-Day Exploit ‘RoguePlanet’ Released
items.description: Exploiting a race condition in Microsoft Defender, the exploit leads to local privilege escalation to SYSTEM. The post New Windows Zero-Day Exploit ‘RoguePlanet’ Released appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft: Some Windows PCs fail to install latest monthly updates
items.description: Microsoft warned customers on Tuesday that they may have issues installing the latest monthly updates on some Windows devices that were upgraded to Windows 11 24H2 or 25H2. [...]

items.title: After AI Reaches Production: 12 Ways Security Teams Can Take Control
items.description: Security teams need more than visibility into AI applications, they need a repeatable framework for monitoring, investigating, and defending them in production. The post After AI Reaches Production: 12 Ways Security Teams Can Take Control appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
items.description: Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem. Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report looks stable. Leadership reads "stable" as "secure." It usually isn't. The work slows down. The risk does not. That gap is what a The Hacker News webinar with Picus Security sets out to close. Autumn

items.title: Microsoft patches YellowKey, GreenPlasma, MiniPlasma zero-days
items.description: On Tuesday, Microsoft patched two zero-day vulnerabilities that let attackers gain SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems, and a third one that grants access to BitLocker-protected drives. [...]

items.title: ServiceNow Patches Vulnerability Exploited Against Some Customers
items.description: The company updated hosted customer instances to patch a security issue it reportedly had known about since April 7. The post ServiceNow Patches Vulnerability Exploited Against Some Customers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
items.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release. Of the 206 flaws, 39 are rated Critical, and 167 are rated Important in severity. This includes 63 privilege escalation, 56 remote code execution, 30 information disclosure, 27 spoofing, 20 security

items.title: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched in Fortinet, Ivanti Products
items.description: Two OS command injection flaws can be exploited remotely, without authentication, for arbitrary code execution. The post Critical Vulnerabilities Patched in Fortinet, Ivanti Products appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: How has use of framing protection security headers changed in the past 3 years?, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: Back in 2023, I wrote a diary[1] discussing how commonly X-Frame-Options and CSP headers containing the frame-ancestors directive were used on 1 million most popular domains on the internet (based on the Tranco list[2]), and how they were set. Given that three years have passed since then, I thought it might be interesting to repeat the analysis and see what â€“ if anything â€“ has changed in the meantime.

items.title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact
items.description: In addition, Rockwell Automation announced some enhancements to its SecureOT cybersecurity solution for OT. The post ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its Most Powerful AI Yet, With Cyber Safeguards
items.description: On June 9, Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, the most capable model it has ever made, generally available. It also did something unusual: it shipped one model as two products, split not by capability but by a layer of safety classifiers. Fable 5 goes to the public. Its twin, Claude Mythos 5, the same underlying model with the cyber safeguards lifted, stays locked to a vetted group of cyber

items.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
items.description: ServiceNow has warned about a security incident in which unknown threat actors exploited a flaw to obtain deeper unauthorized access to susceptible instances. "On June 5, 2026, ServiceNow applied a security update to hosted customer instances," the company revealed in an advisory that requires customer access. "The update concerned a security issue that could allow an unauthenticated user, in

items.title: Ivanti: Max severity Sentry flaw allows code execution as root
items.description: Ivanti has patched two critical vulnerabilities in its Sentry secure mobile gateway solution, including a maximum-severity flaw that enables remote attackers to execute code with root privileges. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Defender RoguePlanet Zero-Day Grants SYSTEM Access on Updated Windows
items.description: The anonymous security researcher going by the name Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for yet another Microsoft Defender zero-day named RoguePlanet. "The exploit is a race condition, so it's a hit or miss," the researcher, who published the exploit under a new GitHub account, "MSNightmare" said. "I have managed to get a 100% success rate on

items.title: Six Proto6 Vulnerabilities in protobuf.js Expose Node.js Apps to RCE and DoS
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged half a dozen vulnerabilities in protobuf.js, a JavaScript and TypeScript implementation of Protocol Buffers (Protobuf), that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution (RCE) and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. "In affected environments, a single malicious protobuf schema, descriptor, or crafted payload could be enough to trigger

items.title: Anthropic rolls out Claude Fable 5, but it's available for a limited time
items.description: Anthropic has begun rolling out a new model called "Fable," which is based on the same underlying model as Mythos, its most powerful AI model class. [...]

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 10th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9966, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Microsoft Defender 'RoguePlanet' zero-day grants SYSTEM privileges
items.description: A security researcher has released a new Microsoft Defender zero-day exploit named "RoguePlanet" just hours after Microsoft fixed two previously disclosed flaws during June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]

items.title: UK weakens proposed telecoms defenses against Chinese hackers after industry pushback
items.description: Britain has weakened proposed cybersecurity protections for its telecoms networks that were developed in response to the Salt Typhoon espionage campaign, after the companies responsible for implementing the measures lobbied against them.

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyber War Is Reshaping Everyday Life
items.description: Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyber attacks threaten hospitals, utilities and essential services.

items.title: Blame AI: Patch Tuesday Hits Record 206 CVEs
items.description: Voluminous patch updates could soon be the norm, as artificial intelligence accelerates the speed and scale of vulnerability discovery.

items.title: ServiceNow discloses security incident exposing customer data
items.description: ServiceNow is warning about a security incident after attackers exploited an unauthenticated access flaw through a vulnerable API endpoint, allowing them to query data from customer instances. [...]

items.title: OpenClaw AI agent found falling for phishing attacks, spills user data
items.description: Phishing simulation on an OpenClaw email agent with various configuration profiles showed that it was susceptible to tactics commonly used to compromise human users. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Exchange Flaw Lets Attackers Spoof Any Email Address
items.description: “Ghost-Sender" is the result of a widespread misconfiguration, according to researchers, and evidence indicates it's being actively abused in the wild.

items.title: SAP fixes critical flaws in NetWeaver and Commerce Cloud
items.description: SAP has released fixes for 15 vulnerabilities as part of its June 2026 Security Patch package, including four critical-severity flaws affecting SAP NetWeaver and SAP Commerce Cloud. [...]

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Burrows Into 73 Microsoft Repositories
items.description: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasmi attack on Microsoft last month.

items.title: CISA to transform how it assesses cyber vulnerabilities and risks, Andersen says
items.description: A binding operational directive being released Wednesday will direct federal agencies to change the way they address vulnerabilities by elevating some while putting others to the side.

items.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.description: Microsoft today released patches for 204 vulnerabilities. 38 of these vulnerabilities are considered critical, and three have been disclosed before today. Six of the vulnerabilities affect Microsoft cloud solutions and do not require any user action. In addition, Microsoft incorporated 360 different vulnerabilities affecting Chromium into its Edge browser.

items.title: Meta to Use Off-Site Business Data for Feed and AI Personalization
items.description: Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond targeted ads. "Businesses often share information about people's activity on their sites with us to make ads more relevant," Meta said in a statement. "We already use this data - like games you play

items.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
items.description: Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup & Replication software that could result in remote code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.4 out of a maximum of 10.0. "A vulnerability allowing remote code execution (RCE) on the Backup Server by an authenticated domain user," Veeam said in a Tuesday advisory. It

items.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
items.description: Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject an information stealer into the code. "Our priority is to protect customers and the broader ecosystem," a Microsoft spokesperson told The Hacker News via email. "We temporarily removed some

items.title: Russian Attackers Weaponize WinRAR Flaw Against Ukrainian Orgs
items.description: Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.

items.title: Hackers pose as women seeking romance to spy on Russian soldiers
items.description: The group, dubbed SiribClone by Russian cybersecurity firm F6, has been active since at least the summer of 2025 and has primarily targeted members of the Russian armed forces stationed in border regions and combat zones.

items.title: WinRAR Flaw Exploited by Russia-Aligned Groups to Deploy Stealers in Ukraine
items.description: Two Russia-aligned cyber attack campaigns have continued to exploit a security flaw in WinRAR to target Ukrainian organisations, almost a year after patches for the vulnerability were released. The activity has been attributed by Trend Micro to Earth Dahu (aka Gamaredon) and SHADOW-EARTH-066 (aka UAC-0226). It involves the exploitation of CVE-2025-8088, a path traversal flaw that allows an

items.title: CISA Announces Winners of the 2026 President’s Cup Cybersecurity Competition
ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 9th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9964, (Tue, Jun 9th)

items.title: AI Slop Will Kill Cybersecurity Storytelling If We Let It
items.description: AI-generated content threatens credibility in cybersecurity. This "Ask the Expert" column explores why human oversight matters and how to maintain authentic narratives.

items.title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
items.description: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.

items.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
items.description: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.

items.title: UK gives big tech 3 months to create device controls to block nude images of kids
items.description: The companies “must activate built-in features or implement technical solutions on smartphones and tablets to detect and block nude images for children,” according to a press release from the Home Office. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced the measure in a speech at London Tech Week Monday.

items.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
items.description: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-06-07, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: This diary continues the Internet Storm Center&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s tracking of the TeamPCP supply chain campaign, first documented in the SANS white paper When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon and most recently in the handler diary Activity Through 2026-05-24. Since that update, the story moved into two new places: the United States government, which formally caught up to the campaign, and the wider population of attackers now wielding the Mini Shai-Hulud framework that TeamPCP open-sourced last month.

items.title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
items.description: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat.

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: The Evil MSI Background is Back!, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: A few months ago, I wrote a diary about a payload that was embedded into a JPEG picture. It was a MSI-branded background[1]. Yesterday, I spotted another one! It seems that the technic is getting more and more popular. This time, it started with a mail containing a WeTransfer link.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, June 5th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9960, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Microsoft's Coreutils for Windows, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;ve been using the GnuWin32 CoreUtils for Windows for many years now (it gives you many *nix core commands on Windows).

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 4th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9958, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
